findings.summary: Component counts, BOM, and hierarchical sheet loading are correct; assembly_complexity.total_components (92) inflated relative to statistics.total_components (62); multi_driver_nets contains false ...

correct.description: 62 total components across parent+child sheets, all refs properly grouped in BOM. 22 decoupling caps on VCC correctly identified with bulk (22uF) and bypass (100nF) coverage. 5 RC filters (200Ω + 330pF, 2.41 MHz) correctly detected — these are SCSI termination networks.

The parent sheet uses J1 for the Apple II bus edge connector (lib: PCM_MA_Connector_AppleII:Bus_Apple_II_Card_Side) and the child sheet main_logic also uses J1 for a SW_DIP_x08. These are genuinely two different physical components sharing the same reference designator — a real design annotation error that the analyzer correctly flags.

Bus detection is accurate for this SCSI card design. Hierarchical label set and bus signals are consistent with an Apple II SCSI card interfacing the Apple II address bus (A0-A10), data bus (D0-D7), buffered SCSI data (B_D0-B_D7), and extended address (EA8-EA13). The bus 'width' values (e.g., 45 for A prefix) represent entry counts across both sheets, not signal count.

incorrect.description: The assembly_complexity section reports 92 total components and 87 SMD, but statistics.total_components is 62 with 56 missing footprints. The overcounting of ~30 components likely comes from including power symbols or hierarchical instances multiple times. Additionally, 86 components classified as 'unknown' package are counted as SMD 'medium' difficulty — components with no footprint should not be counted as SMD.

Multiple nets report the same component/pin pair twice as separate drivers, e.g., net '__unnamed_19' has U2B pin 11 listed twice with identical data. This appears to be a parser bug where split-unit symbols register the same output pin multiple times when both gate halves share a net. These are false positive multi-driver warnings.

D1 (1N914, footprint Diode_THT:D_DO-35...), RP1/RP2 (3.3K, R_Array_SIP10), U3A (2764/27128, DIP-28), and U5C (6264, DIP-28) all have footprints assigned but are listed in missing_mpn. This is technically correct — they are missing MPN (part number), not footprint. However, the missing_mpn list is titled as such and is accurate. No error here on reflection.

findings.summary: decoupling_analysis correctly returns 0 items for subsheet with only RC-filter capacitors; ERC warnings correctly identify 12 undriven nets (A0-A9, ~{RES}, ~{IOSEL}) in the subsheet; PWR_FLAG warni...

correct.description: The 5 capacitors in main_logic (C10, C14, C15, C16, C7 — all 330pF) are connected to signal pins forming RC filters, not to VCC. The analyzer correctly identifies 0 decoupling caps in this subsheet and instead flags the relevant ICs (U3A, U3E, U3C, etc.) in design_observations as lacking local decoupling — those caps exist in the parent sheet.

These are Apple II bus signals (address lines, control signals) that arrive via hierarchical labels from the parent sheet. In the subsheet context they have no local driver — this is an accurate ERC observation. The parent sheet shows these same hierarchical labels as 'unconnected_hierarchical', confirming they are driven externally.

The subsheet has power pins for VCC and GND but no PWR_FLAG symbol or power_out driver within its scope. These are valid ERC warnings for a subsheet analyzed in isolation. The parent sheet correctly shows 0 PWR_FLAG warnings because the connector P2 provides power_out context there.

incorrect.description: The SW_DIP_x08 DIP switch component is categorized as type 'connector' in both the BOM and component_types. A DIP switch is a switch/mechanical component, not a connector. The analyzer appears to classify it as connector because the KiCad library symbol is in the 'Switch' library but the value doesn't match IC/resistor/capacitor/diode patterns. This misclassification also affects the component_types count.

missed.description: The NCR 5380 is a dedicated SCSI bus controller IC. The analyzer's memory_interfaces and bus_analysis detectors return empty, missing the opportunity to identify this as a SCSI bus interface. The B_D0-B_D7 bus connecting the 5380, ROM (U3A), RAM (U5C), and bus buffer (U4E) is a classic SCSI data path that could be flagged as a memory/peripheral bus topology.
